questions.question: Use {a1} to ask about a person.
In most wh-questions, the wh-word comes {a1}.
Use {a1} to ask about a place.
After a wh-word, many questions need an {a1}.
The subject usually comes after the {a1}.
With to be, the form of be comes before the {a1}.
Correct form: How {a1} money do we need?
With perfect tenses, have/has/had comes before the {a1}.
{a1} will the committee publish its findings?
{a1} would the board reject that option?
{a1} should we invite to the review?
How {a1} datasets were excluded from the model?
Object question: Who {a1} the panel nominate?
Correct form: Why {a1} the results changed?
Use {a1} to ask about an amount of uncountable money.
Subject questions are the main exception to wh + {a1} + subject.

saved_test.name: Wh-Questions (All levels V3)
saved_test.description: Standard V3 package for Wh-Questions across A1-C2.
